title: NGE on list of best anime of all time
display_title: NGE on list of best anime of all time
wikitext:
[]: NGE on list of best anime of all time...             Quote Post by Rightstated » Tue Apr 17, 2012 11:39 am So, I'm big on reading lots of manga, and watching lots of new anime, and I'm always looking for input on the next thing to watch/read. I want it to be worth my while however, so whenever I scour the internet or ask for people's opinions, I obviously ask them or look for, what they thing the best of all time is. NGE is consistently at the top of the list (maybe in the top 10, etc...), and on my list, out of the 50 or so manga/anime I have watched in entirety of my life, NGE is undoubtedly the number 1 slot on my list, and I don't see it ever changing. Because I always like hearing what other people think of NGE, I will constantly search different databases that review and rank anime, and people's personal online lists of what they think is the best anime ever. I know that this is a completely subjective question, but I feel like NGE always deserves the number 1 spot on any list, and if it isn't I get really puzzled, especially when other, it seems obviously more crappy anime, is ranked higher than NGE. I was on a website the other day... here is the link: http://www.themanime.org/reviewlist.php ... and it had multiple reviews of NGE, and I believe two of the reviews gave it 3 stars out of 5. And End of Evangelion was given 2 stars out of 5!!! I was thus enraged at such a comment, and it was baffling to see that somebody didn't share my same opinion. I figured it was because he was just ignorant or something along the lines of... (This is slightly funny and ironic, because my mindset is the same that ignorance is often derived... but still) ... Anyways, I think it might have to do somewhat with this "hipster" culture, who refuses to like things that other people like (even if they actually like it) just for the sake of being different. And I'm not saying that you should conform either, but saying shows like Gurren Lagan (though good), are better than NGE, I find to be absurd. Being different for the sake of being different and being the same for the sake of being the same, I both find to be wrong. Trying to put myself in the shoes of these people, I thought that people might see NGE as a thing which unnecessarily tries to be more complex than it needs to be (which I certainly don't agree with because every question asked maintains relevance and importance to the general flow of the story ... in my mind). Also I think that people might not just like it because they are too ignorant to contemplate the various questions presented, but then again, I do not know. Or maybe people were fed up due to the way the show ended and was carried out, and due to spite, gave it a bad review. .. Anyways, any thoughts on the matter would be appreciated, and maybe an insight to where NGE falls on your list of to anime/manga. ---> (I promise that I won't be offended, I was just in a blind an ignorant rage that made me lose track of sensibility... haha) Thanks all.

[]:             Quote Post by Jayfive » Tue Apr 17, 2012 12:04 pm Understanding Im only saying this because im actually interested in reading your post but - paragraphs can be your friend :)

[]:             Quote Post by Final Messenger » Tue Apr 17, 2012 1:36 pm Saying that Evangelion should be on everybody’s top ten anime lists is like me saying Persona 4 should be on everyone’s top ten video games lists. Everybody has a different opinion nothing is going to be universally liked or hated. Some things just don’t jive well with others, an example I personally don’t really care for Gundam Seed Destiny but a friend of mine thinks it is the best Gundam. Is he wrong? No, am I wrong? No. Just because I don’t care for Seed Destiny does not mean my friend's opinion of it is invalid. So if some people don’t like Evangelion that does not mean they are wrong it just means they don’t like Evangelion simple as that.

[]: Re: NGE on list of best anime of all time...             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Tue Apr 17, 2012 1:38 pm Paragraphs have been added at approximately logical locations, given the run-on sentences. The reasons for not liking NGE or EoE are quite obvious, starting with the fact that Shinji performs above and beyond the level of betafaggotry expected of a harem lead, and then totally loses the plot in EoE, letting the best girl get kerb-stomped in the process. This sort of thought process: Another NGE copypasta wrote: Hi, my name is Shinji Ikari. I am a cipher, a symbol, a representation of human futility and impotency--creative impotency, I mean. Thanks to the narrow-minded dedication of the director and his writing staff, I am the locus of a squandered plot. I anthropomorphize failed character development. When you loathe me, it's supposed to be art. But let's be honest, that's bullshit spread over a sewage sandwich. From a characterological perspective, the climax fails spectacularly. The primary conflicts are completely abandoned in exchange for a philosophically shallow, symbolically incoherent ending. Most of the other protagonists are killed or driven nuts, because death is easier to write than emotional maturation. And I think our budget was running out. Did you know that a hentai circle produced a doujin series with a better ending? Yeah. I'm sorry. At least you can pretend that I scored the emotionally vulnerable German chick. I mean, shooting fish in a barrel, and somehow I missed...

[]:             Quote Post by Defectron » Tue Apr 17, 2012 2:03 pm And I'm not saying that you should conform either, but saying shows like Gurren Lagan (though good), are better than NGE, I find to be absurd. Being different for the sake of being different and being the same for the sake of being the same, I both find to be wrong.

[]:             Quote Post by Fireball » Tue Apr 17, 2012 6:06 pm NGE isn't perfect. And while I do think it speaks to quite a lot of people and in a very special manner, there are a great number it will not touch upon. Not everyone can enjoy Shinji & Co, not everyone will appreciate the artsiness or get behind the convoluted plot. There are a thousand reasons why one might not like it. However, I do think that for all its faults it did something else so incredibly good that it compensates in the long run. Is it the best? Who knows. Does it matter? Not really. All I know is that I've never been touched as much by any other anime as I was with Eva, that's why it will always hold a special place in my heart.

[]:             Quote Post by Henry Spencer » Tue Apr 17, 2012 7:02 pm If you looked closer at the EoE review, you would've seen that the reviewer still recommended that people who watched the series should watch it for themselves.

[]: WATCH TAMERS! BD             Quote Post by Alaska Slim » Wed Apr 18, 2012 12:35 am Final Messenger wrote: Saying that Evangelion should be on everybody’s top ten anime lists is like me saying Persona 4 should be on everyone’s top ten video games lists.

[]: Re: WATCH TAMERS! BD             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Wed Apr 18, 2012 1:39 am Alaska Slim wrote: Professor, WHY ISN'T [ Digimon Tamers ] ON YOUR LIST?!?

[]: Re: WATCH TAMERS! BD             Quote Post by Final Messenger » Wed Apr 18, 2012 6:58 am Alaska Slim wrote: Professor, WHY ISN'T IT ON YOUR LIST?!?

[]:             Quote Post by Tankred » Wed Apr 18, 2012 7:50 am 'You should like what I like, or you're a faggot/hipster' argument, nice try, OP.

[]: Re: NGE on list of best anime of all time...             Quote Post by Bagheera » Wed Apr 18, 2012 11:48 am Mr. Tines wrote: The reasons for not liking NGE or EoE are quite obvious, starting with the fact that Shinji performs above and beyond the level of betafaggotry expected of a harem lead, and then totally loses the plot in EoE, letting the best girl get kerb-stomped in the process. This sort of thought process:

[]:             Quote Post by gatotsu911 » Wed Apr 18, 2012 5:01 pm Eva obviously isn't to everyone's tastes, but if you're compiling a list of the "greatest" anime of all time - measured at least in part in terms of semi-objective factors like critical reception, cultural impact, originality, influence on the medium and quality of craftsmanship - Eva does sort of seem like a glaring omission. I don't have any great personal enthusiasm for Cowboy Bebop or the works of Miyazaki, for example, but I'd be remiss not to include them on a list of "great" anime.

[]:             Quote Post by Bagheera » Thu Apr 19, 2012 10:16 am gatotsu911 wrote: Eva obviously isn't to everyone's tastes, but if you're compiling a list of the "greatest" anime of all time - measured at least in part in terms of semi-objective factors like critical reception, cultural impact, originality, influence on the medium and quality of craftsmanship - Eva does sort of seem like a glaring omission. I don't have any great personal enthusiasm for Cowboy Bebop or the works of Miyazaki, for example, but I'd be remiss not to include them on a list of "great" anime.

[]:             Quote Post by Redaye » Thu Apr 26, 2012 1:52 am It gets my vote not nearly as much for its conscious appeal as its subconscious. Something in NGE grabbed the tragedies in my long past and shook them to the surface. I haven't seen another series of late that has that exact ability. It was realizing I will always choose dark chocolate to any other flavor, despite them all being good to some degree.

[]: Re: WATCH TAMERS! BD             Quote Post by Alaska Slim » Thu Apr 26, 2012 3:45 am Mr. Tines wrote: It pre-dated my (re-)discovery of anime, never obviously presented itself to me, and by now, Narutaru has pretty much done over the mon thing for me.

[]:             Quote Post by bobbyfischer's ghost » Wed May 02, 2012 11:42 am Personally I love Eva and it's on my list but I think it's impossible to construct a list that's not subjective.

[]: Re: WATCH TAMERS! BD             Quote Post by Henry Spencer » Wed May 02, 2012 5:10 pm Alaska Slim wrote: And is also less crap-sack than the former, as it was intended for kids.

[]:             Quote Post by robersora » Wed May 02, 2012 7:25 pm I personally would put it on the list of the best Anime of all time, but I can understand if someone wouldn't. After all, such lists are always quite subjective. But what nobody can deny is the fact, that Evangelion is one of the most influential Anime of all time. But that is a different topic.

[]:             Quote Post by sum_exsanguis » Wed May 02, 2012 7:42 pm Let us also be honest. It takes someone of relatively decent intelligence to comprehend eva. You have to have either the knowledge of religion and psychology or have the want to go find more, and honestly people do not want to do work anymore. Eva had a deeper but still personal meaning to anyone who is willing to look for it, but to only take it at face value; you would never like it.
